title: juncus emmanuelis
intro: Juncus emmanuelis, also known as Devil's Tail Grass, is a type of flowering plant native to New Zealand and other parts of the Pacific Islands. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to aid in wound healing and the treatment of various skin conditions. The health benefits of Juncus emmanuelis include its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections, making it a useful natural remedy for conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, Juncus emmanuelis is often used in the form of a tincture or tea, and can also be applied topically as a poultice or ointment to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
